summary: Cryptographic Signing Layer (ECDSA P-256 + SHA-256) testing completed. Backend: 6/6 tests passed. Frontend: All features verified - 'Cryptographically Verified' badge displays correctly with algorithm name. Fixed minor UX issue where old stamps incorrectly showed 'Signature Invalid' badge.
critical_code_review_comments: CryptoSigningService properly loads keys from TLS_PRIVATE_KEY_B64 and TLS_PUBLIC_KEY_B64 env vars
ECDSA P-256 + SHA-256 correctly implemented for stamp signing and verification
Public key endpoint at /.well-known/tls-stamp-keys returns proper JWKS-like response with PEM key
Crypto signature is added to stamps on creation via crypto_service.sign_stamp()
Verification endpoint correctly returns crypto_verified=true/false/null based on signature status
updated_files: /app/frontend/src/pages/VerifyPage.jsx - Fixed crypto badge display logic for old stamps
seed_data_creation: None - used existing stamps including newly created crypto stamp TLS-20260225-F3817F1F
context_for_next_testing_agent: Crypto signing layer fully implemented. Key endpoints: GET /api/crypto/status (returns signing_available=true, algorithm=ECDSA_P256_SHA256), GET /api/.well-known/tls-stamp-keys (returns public key PEM), POST /api/documents/stamp (adds crypto_signature), GET /api/verify/stamp/{id} (includes crypto_verified field). Test stamps: TLS-20260225-F3817F1F (crypto_verified=true), TLS-20260225-0DEF71F8 (crypto_verified=null - old stamp). Frontend shows blue 'Cryptographically Verified (ECDSA_P256_SHA256)' badge only for crypto_verified=true stamps.

features_verified.GET /api/.well-known/tls-stamp-keys: PASS - Returns public key in PEM format with proper JWKS-like structure
features_verified.POST /api/documents/stamp - crypto signa: PASS - New stamps created with crypto_signature field
features_verified.Frontend - Cryptographically Verified ba: PASS - Shows for crypto_verified=true stamps with algorithm name
features_verified.Frontend - No badge for old stamps: PASS - After fix, old stamps without crypto don't show misleading badge

crypto_badge_null_handling.issue: Old stamps with crypto_verified=null showed 'Signature Invalid' badge due to truthy/falsy check
crypto_badge_null_handling.fix: Changed condition from 'crypto_verified !== undefined' to explicit 'crypto_verified === true' and 'crypto_verified === false' checks
